rationale: The cited paper is used as the source of measurement items in the citing paper's follow-up analysis, where the authors note that satisfaction and return-intention items were adapted from Shah et al. (2014). This is a concrete methodological role rather than a generic background mention. At the same time, the citation is peripheral to the core traffic-light aggregation theory and main calorie-ordering tests, so its substantive role is narrow.
relation_description: The citing paper borrows and applies survey item wording from Shah et al. (2014) to assess customer reactions (e.g., meal enjoyment, perceived restaurant concern, return intentions) in a supplemental study. The cited work functions as a measurement source rather than a core theoretical foundation for the main hypotheses.
